agent_output: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"Critical IDOR vulnerability: authenticated users can access any delivery by ID, and the public location endpoint enables unrestricted enumeration of delivery data."},"critical_findings":[{"title":"Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) on /deliveries/:id","risk":"Any authenticated user can read any other user's delivery by guessing or enumerating IDs. No ownership check exists between req.user and the delivery record.","severity":"Critical","location":"delivery-routes.ts:4-6 (GET /deliveries/:id handler)","reproduction":"1. Authenticate as User A. 2. GET /deliveries/<User B's delivery ID> with User A's token. 3. Receive User B's full delivery payload.","fix":"After findById, verify delivery.userId === req.user.id (or equivalent ownership/role check). Return 403 if mismatch. Example: `if (!delivery || delivery.userId !== req.user.id) return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Forbidden' });`","time_to_fix":"15 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Data isolation between customers; OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control"},{"title":"Public location endpoint exposes delivery existence and location without any auth or rate limit","risk":"GET /deliveries/:id/location requires no authentication. An attacker can enumerate delivery IDs to map all active delivery locations in real time — a privacy and physical-security risk for drivers and recipients.","severity":"Critical","location":"delivery-routes.ts:8-12 (GET /deliveries/:id/location handler)","reproduction":"1. Script a loop: for id in 1..100000, GET /deliveries/{id}/location. 2. Collect all non-null responses — gives real-time GPS of every active delivery.","fix":"Either (a) require auth + ownership check like the other route, or (b) if public tracking is a product requirement, gate on an unguessable tracking token (e.g., /track/:trackingToken/location) and add rate limiting.","time_to_fix":"30 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Customer and driver privacy; API abuse prevention"},{"title":"JWT_SECRET non-null assertion crashes the process when env var is missing","risk":"process.env.JWT_SECRET! will be undefined if the env var is unset (e.g., misconfigured deploy, new environment). jwt.verify with an undefined secret throws a JsonWebTokenError, meaning ALL authenticated requests 401 — a full auth outage, not a graceful degradation.","severity":"High","location":"auth-middleware.ts:8","reproduction":"1. Deploy without JWT_SECRET in the environment. 2. Any request to an auth-protected route returns 401. 3. No log or startup error indicates the root cause.","fix":"Validate JWT_SECRET at startup (fail fast): `if (!process.env.JWT_SECRET) throw new Error('JWT_SECRET is required');` — outside the request handler, at module load time.","time_to_fix":"5 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Deployment reliability; zero-downtime deploys"}],"important_issues":[{"title":"No null check on delivery lookup result","description":"db.deliveries.findById(req.params.id) may return null/undefined for a non-existent ID. The handler blindly passes the result to res.json(), which sends `null` with a 200 status instead of a proper 404. This leaks information about valid vs invalid IDs and confuses API consumers.","location":"delivery-routes.ts:5"},{"title":"Same null-check gap on getLocation","description":"db.deliveries.getLocation() likely returns null for non-existent deliveries. Sending null as 200 makes it impossible for consumers to distinguish 'no location yet' from 'delivery does not exist'.","location":"delivery-routes.ts:11"},{"title":"No async error handling","description":"Both route handlers use async but have no try/catch or express-async-errors wrapper. A DB failure will produce an unhandled promise rejection instead of a 500 response.","location":"delivery-routes.ts:4-12"}],"suggestions":["Add an express async error handler or use a wrapper like express-async-errors to catch unhandled rejections in route handlers","Consider using UUIDs instead of sequential IDs for deliveries to reduce enumeration risk even behind auth","Add request logging/audit trail for delivery access to detect unauthorized access attempts"],"production_readiness":{"deployment_risks":"If JWT_SECRET env var is missing or rotated without restarting all instances, auth breaks silently for all users. No startup validation catches this.","rollback_capability":"No schema or data changes — pure application code. Rollback is safe.","backward_compatibility":"No breaking API contract changes. Adding auth to the location endpoint (recommended fix) would be a breaking change for existing consumers — coordinate with frontend/mobile.","monitoring_coverage":"No mention of logging, metrics, or alerting. Auth failures and delivery access should emit structured logs for security monitoring.","breaking_changes":{"api_breaking":false,"db_destructive":false,"shared_library":false,"auth_security":true,"data_pipeline":false,"config_changes":false,"details":["IDOR allows cross-tenant data access — this is a live security vulnerability","Public endpoint leaks real-time delivery locations without authentication"]},"safe_to_deploy":false,"rollback_plan":"Revert to previous version. No data migration involved."},"escalations":[{"issue":"IDOR vulnerability may already be exploitable in production","reason":"If this code pattern exists in the current production deployment, customer delivery data is already exposed to any authenticated user. This needs immediate security review beyond code review scope.","routed_to":"Coach K / Security Team"}],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["IDOR vulnerability is definitively present — no ownership check exists","JWT_SECRET crash path is deterministic given missing env var","Public location endpoint has zero access control"],"low_confidence_areas":["Cannot verify if a global auth/ownership middleware exists upstream that we don't see in these two files","Cannot verify if rate limiting exists at infrastructure level (API gateway, CDN)"],"assumptions":["These files represent the complete middleware chain for these routes — no upstream ownership check exists","db.deliveries.findById returns null for missing records rather than throwing"]}}

No ownership check exists between req.user and the delivery record.","severity":"Critical","location":

content.thinking:
Let me analyze these two files for critical issues.

Key hypotheses:
1. Missing authorization check - authenticated user can access ANY delivery, not just their own (IDOR)
2. JWT_SECRET from env could be undefined (non-null assertion)
3. Public location endpoint has no rate limiting - enumeration risk
4. No null check on delivery lookup - could return null/undefined as JSON

Let me produce the output.
